ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Agricultural Meteorologists analyze weather patterns and climate trends to predict crop yields, advise farmers on optimal planting and harvesting times, and develop strategies to mitigate the impact of extreme weather events on agriculture.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Agricultural Meteorologists work with farmers, researchers, and policymakers to understand the complex relationships between weather, climate, and agricultural productivity. They use advanced statistical models, remote sensing technologies, and field observations to provide actionable insights and recommendations that help farmers make informed decisions and improve crop yields.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: M.Sc. in Meteorology or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in pursuing a career in Agricultural Meteorology can start by taking courses in mathematics, physics, and computer programming.
target_degrees: B.Sc. in Meteorology or related field

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree in a related field (e.g., B.Sc. in Physics or Mathematics) can pursue a postgraduate degree in Meteorology or a related field to transition into this role.

future_builder.description: Agricultural Meteorologists with 2-5 years of experience can advance to senior roles by developing expertise in specialized areas such as climate modeling, remote sensing, or agricultural policy.

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into Agricultural Meteorology by acquiring relevant skills and experience. No degree is required, but a strong portfolio of projects and certifications is essential.
survival_skills_needed: Programming skills in Python or R
portfolio_proof_required: Project on crop yield prediction using machine learning
Case study on climate change impact on agriculture

roadmap.title: Data Analysis and Visualization
topics: Data analysis using Python or R
Data visualization using ArcGIS or QGIS

roadmap.title: Capstone Project and Portfolio Development
topics: Crop yield prediction using machine learning
Case study on climate change impact on agriculture
